prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: full-screen game background plate for an interactive browser railway simulator
Primary request: Create a polished dense isometric pixel-art railway operations map of the real Tokaido Shinkansen corridor across central Japan, Tokyo and Yokohama at the right/east side, Nagoya in the middle, Kyoto and Shin-Osaka at the left/west side.
Scene/backdrop: recognizable Japanese coastline, bays, rivers, bridges, hills, forested mountains, dense city districts around each station, station yards, elevated rail viaducts, multiple parallel tracks, roads, canals, small boats, parks, and compact buildings.
Subject: the rail infrastructure and city environment only. The image should be an empty interactive game board waiting for train sprites.
Style/medium: crisp 2D isometric pixel-art, late-1990s Japanese railway management sim, A-Train / SimCity operations screen level of detail, hand-painted pixel texture, readable miniature world.
Composition/framing: wide landscape map, rail corridor clearly follows left-to-right across the middle, several stations visible as detailed rail yards, no UI panels inside the image, no title.
Lighting/mood: calm sunny morning, cozy ASMR rail ambience, soft but detailed.
Color palette: rich ocean blues, lush greens, concrete grays, muted city colors, warm amber platform lights.
Critical constraints: NO large train in the background, NO oversized train, NO foreground train, NO readable text, NO labels, NO UI panels, NO logos, NO watermark, NO checkerboard. Tiny parked trains may appear only as small details in station yards, but the main track must be clear for interactive sprites.
